title: Bacterial Transformation - Mix & Go Competent Cells - CHEM 584
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3tffr","text":"Get an aliquot of the Mix \u0026 Go competent cells from the -80 Freezer (ask a TA to get them for you)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"53bnr","text":"You should follow standard BSL-1 procedures when using this protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 1 µL to 5 µL plasmid DNA to a 50 µL aliqanuot of thawed competent cells (Mix & Go) on ice, mix gently for a few seconds (try to keep the added volume of DNA less than 5% of the total). Place tube on ice for 00:05:00 minutes. When selecting with Kanamycin, Tetracycline, etc., an outgrowth performed in SOC medium is required for efficient transformation. After the transformation mixture has incubated on ice for at least 5 min, add 4 volumes of SOC (e.g., 400 μl of SOC to 100 μl of the transformation mixture) and incubate for 1 hour at 37°C with gentle shaking at 200-300 rpm.
Spread 50 µL to 100 µL of the mixture onto a pre-warmed (37°C) LB plate containing Ampicillin (or other selection antibiotic). Using chilled LB plates will decrease the transformation efficiency.

Incubate overnight at 37 °C in the bacterial incubator. You can also incubate on the bench or in a drawer at room temperature for a longer time if this is convenient for scheduling. For example, leaving a plate on the bench for 2-3 days at room temperature usually generates nice sized colonies and you avoid having to come into the lab the next day (e.g., over the weekend).
Prewarm culture plates in a 37 °C incubator for 00:30:00 .
creator: Ken Christensen
